name: Apple-Walnut Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. In a work bowl, process flour, cold butter, Crisco, and salt until the butter is in pea-size pieces.
2. Gradually add water (or less) while processing on-off turns with the mixer only until the dough begins to hold its shape.
3. Shape the dough into a flat circle, wrap it, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Roll out the dough on a floured surface and transfer it to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom. Cover with foil and weight with beans. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 10 minutes or until golden brown.
5. Remove the beans, let cool.
6. In a saucepan, mix sugar and water and bring to a boil. Add sliced apples and cook, covered, stirring occasionally, until tender. Drain and cool.
7. In a double boiler, mix sugar, spices, salt, and butter. Quickly add beaten eggs and beat with a whisk until blended. Cook over hot water stirring strongly until thickened. Cool.
8. Arrange apples in the pie shell and pour the mixture over them. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Serves 8.
